title: Number of new state-based conflicts
subtitle: Included are [interstate](#dod:interstate-ucdp), [intrastate](#dod:intrastate-ucdp), and [extrasystemic](#dod:extrasystemic-ucdp) conflicts that started that year.
note: Some conflicts affect several regions, and do not necessarily start at the same time across them. The sum across all regions can therefore be higher than the global number.
caption:
Number of new state-based conflicts

Included are interstate, intrastate, and extrasystemic conflicts that started that year.

This stacked bar chart displays the annual count of new state-based conflicts worldwide from 1946 to 2024, categorized by type. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 18, while the horizontal axis marks years in five-year increments. The legend identifies four conflict types: Internationalized intrastate (orange), Non-internationalized intrastate (green), Extrasystemic (maroon), and Interstate (blue). The data shows significant fluctuation over the period, with two distinct peaks reaching the chart's maximum value of 18. The first peak occurs in 1991, composed primarily of a large green segment for non-internationalized intrastate conflicts topped by an orange segment for internationalized intrastate conflicts. A second peak of 18 appears in 2016, similarly driven by a substantial green base and a large orange upper section. Other notable years include 1946 and 1976, where totals reach approximately 11 and 10 respectively, dominated by green bars. In contrast, the mid-2000s show lower activity, with several years recording totals of 3 or fewer. Blue bars representing interstate conflicts appear intermittently, often forming the base of the stacks in earlier decades like the 1940s and 1970s, while maroon bars for extrasystemic conflicts are visible mainly in the 1950s and 1960s. The note clarifies that because some conflicts affect multiple regions and may not start simultaneously across them, the sum across all regions can be higher than the global number presented here.
